vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Supports wing spar, avionics bay, and front GPS boom mount; provides structural integrity for V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear
CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window
Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable
Urban delivery, noise-constrained rotors, redundant tilt servos, obstacle avoidance pod

risk.desc: Thermal management of avionics bay may be insufficient in high ambient temperatures
risk.mit: Implement active cooling systems or increase thermal insulation

risk.desc: Potential for water ingress through belly camera window
risk.mit: Add gasket or sealant around camera window

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance between wing spar socket and fuselage spine may cause interference
risk.mit: Increase clearance between wing spar socket and fuselage spine

risk.desc: Landing gear mount may not provide sufficient support in high-G maneuvers
risk.mit: Reinforce landing gear mount or redesign landing gear attachment

risk.desc: Front GPS boom mount may be susceptible to vibrations from wing spar loads
risk.mit: Add damping material or redesign GPS mount for better isolation

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Supports wing spar, avionics bay, and front GPS boom mount; provides structural integrity for V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear

physics_paths.description: Transfers nacelle loads into fuselage through spar_socket
Heat dissipation from avionics components into fuselage structure
Transmits pitch and yaw control forces into fuselage
Provides access for camera installation and maintenance
Data and power routing from avionics to fuselage spine

integration_opportunities: Fuselage_spine can be integrated with wing spar for load transfer
Avionics_bay can be integrated with fuselage_spine for mounting avionics components
Front_gps_boom_mount can be integrated with fuselage_spine for GPS boom installation

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.support: Use support structures for overhangs and complex geometries
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Use CMM for dimensional inspection and visual inspection for surface finish

risk.desc: Potential material fatigue due to cyclic loading from rotor thrust and tilt servos.
risk.mit: Conduct fatigue testing and incorporate appropriate material selection and design features.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion causing misalignment of critical joints.
risk.mit: Design with thermal expansion in mind and include expansion joints or flexible connections.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference due to proximity of wiring harness to high-voltage components.
risk.mit: Ensure proper insulation and spacing of wiring harness to minimize risk of electrical interference.

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress through service access panels.
risk.mit: Design service access panels with appropriate sealing and drainage features.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to inadequate fastener preload.
risk.mit: Ensure proper torque settings and use locking mechanisms to prevent loosening.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on aluminum components due to extreme temperature ranges.
risk.mit: Use thermal analysis to validate material selection and consider heat sinks or insulation.

risk.desc: Titanium pushrod may require special handling during assembly to avoid damage.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly instructions and train personnel on proper handling techniques.

risk.desc: Servo mounting may experience preload issues if not properly torqued.

risk.desc: Electrical harness routing may be compromised if not properly secured.
risk.mit: Ensure harness is routed through designated channels and secured with appropriate fasteners.

risk.desc: Wing spar socket may not align properly with wing structure if datum is not controlled.
risk.mit: Define primary datum feature and ensure it is referenced in assembly sequence.
